items.title: Hackers Actively Exploiting SonicWall SMA1000 0-Day Vulnerability in the Wild
items.description:
SonicWall disclosed two vulnerabilities affecting its SMA1000 Series remote access appliances, and threat actors were already exploiting one of them before the advisory even went public. The flaws include a critical server-side request forgery (SSRF) bug, CVE-2026-15409, scoring a perfect 10.0 on the CVSS scale, and a high-severity local privilege escalation flaw, CVE-2026-15410. Both have [] 
 The post Hackers Actively Exploiting SonicWall SMA1000 0-Day Vulnerability in the Wild appear
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] THREAT-INTEL advisory. Risk score 8.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items.ioc_note: Actor-attribution campaign — IOC set derived from threat actor research, not automated feed extraction.

apex.ai_summary: [P1] Vulnerability - critical severity (Risk 9.0/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 2 indicators detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: CRITICAL PRIORITY - Patch within 24-48 hours. Activate IR playbook. Notify SOC lead and CISO.

ioc_paywall.message: 2 IOC(s) at 14.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.kev_name: SonicWall SMA1000 Appliances Server-Side Request Forgery Vulnerability
items.kev_action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISA’s BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISA’s “Forensics Triage Requirements” (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.
items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Hackers Actively Exploiting SonicWall SMA1000 0-Day Vulnerability in the Wild
id: 900f916b-07e6-5402-b85e-965649b19060
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-15409 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: KEV_EXPLOIT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-15409
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/16
modified: 2026/07/16
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-15409 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-16T17:27:27Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-15409", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-15409 Exploitation Attempt - KEV_EXPLOIT"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:9004201; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-16, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Intelligence confirmed: Item is listed in CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities; Active exploitation language detected in intelligence text; CVSS score 10.0 (Critical)

confidence_factors.signal: Item is listed in CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities
Active exploitation language detected in intelligence text

items.recommended_sla_action: EMERGENCY PATCH — Active exploitation confirmed or KEV listed
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-45646 Allocation of resources without limits or throttling in ASPNET Co
id: 7572430f-1602-5eca-a204-0fce42ae1951
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-45646 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-45646
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/16
modified: 2026/07/16
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-45646 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-16T17:27:27Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-45646", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-45646 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:6735957; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-16, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: High confidence based on: CVSS score 9.8 (Critical); EPSS score 0.797 (very high exploitation probability)
items.recommended_sla_action: PRIORITY PATCH — High severity with confirmed threat activity
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2026-15701: A weakness has been identified in Totolink NR1800X 9.1.0u.6279_B20210910. Affected by this issue is the function Form_Lo
items.description: A weakness has been identified in Totolink NR1800X 9.1.0u.6279_B20210910. Affected by this issue is the function Form_Logout of the file /formLogout.htm of the component lighttpd. This manipulation of the argument Host causes stack-based buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-48564 Heap-based buffer overflow in Windows DHCP Server allows an autho
id: 2e3f7398-7d8a-5e3a-a180-365a67f8c0e7
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-48564 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-48564
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/16
modified: 2026/07/16
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: high
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-48564 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-16T17:27:27Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-48564", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-48564 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:1686653; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-16, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: High confidence based on: CVSS score 8.8 (High); EPSS score 0.763 (very high exploitation probability)
items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.
